name: Pork Sate with Peanut Dipping Sauce Vmxv03a
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup plus 1 tablespoon fresh lemongrass (from the bottom of a 6-inch stalk)
3/4 pound pork tenderloin, trimmed and cut into 3" x 1.5" x 0.25" pieces
1/2 cup creamy peanut butter (not old-fashioned or freshly ground)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the skewers in water for 30 minutes.
2. Mix lemongrass, lime juice, and sugar with soy sauce and garlic in a large bowl.
3. Add pork to the mixture and marinate for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
4. Mix remaining lemongrass, lime juice, sugar, and broth with peanut butter, onion, coriander, and jalapeno in a heavy saucepan.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil, whisking frequently.
6. Transfer the mixture to a blender and puree.
7. Strain the purée into a small saucepan, pressing on solids with the back of a spoon.
8. Discard the solids.
9. Simmer until reduced to a thick sauce consistency, stirring frequently, about 6 minutes.
- This can be prepared up to one day in advance. Cover and refrigerate. Reheat before serving and thin with more broth if necessary.
10. Preheat the broiler (or grill).
11. Thread 3 pork pieces onto each skewer.
12. Broil until cooked through, about 3 minutes per side.
13. Pour the sauce into a bowl and place in the center of the platter.
14. Sprinkle with chopped green onion tops.
15. Surround the pork skewers and serve.
